perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall mean a company appointed by Licensee and agreed by Licensor in a country of the Territory for the import, selling, promotion and distribution of the Licensed Product in such country of the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor shall mean a company appointed by Licensee in a country of the Territory for the import, selling, promotion and distribution of the Licensed Product in such country of the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines a distributor as a company appointed by the Licensee and agreed by the Licensor. The changed definition removes the requirement for the Licensor's agreement. This creates a contradiction with Section 2.2. where it says that Licensee may extend the rights granted herein to register, import, export, store, handle, commercialize, promote, distribute and sell to its Affiliates and to its Distributors, provided that Licensee shall first provide to Licensor a written assurance from each of such Affiliate or Distributor to agree to be bound by, and to strictly comply with, all applicable terms, conditions, and obligations in this Agreement towards Licensor. This is a In-Text contradiction because the Licensee can appoint any 'Distributor' that doesn't has to be agreed by the Licensor, but this same Distributor needs to comply with all applicable terms, conditions, and obligations in this Agreement towards Licensor, which makes no sense. This change creates uncertainty regarding the Licensor's control over the distribution network and could lead to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee may extend the rights granted herein to register, import, export, store, handle, commercialize, promote, distribute and sell to its Affiliates and to its Distributors, provided that Licensee shall first provide to Licensor a written assurance from each of such Affiliate or Distributor to agree to be bound by, and to strictly comply with, all applicable terms, conditions, and obligations in this Agreement towards Licensor. Licensee shall not be entitled to assign the License or any of its rights under this Agreement or to grant any sub-licenses.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for the Licensor's agreement in the appointment of a distributor. The contradicted text states that any distributor needs to comply with all applicable terms, conditions, and obligations in this Agreement towards Licensor. This is a clear contradiction because without the Licensor agreeing to the distributor in the first place, it is unclear how or why that distributor would be bound to the Licensor under the agreement, thus making the requirement for compliance nonsensical and potentially unenforceable. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: License shall have the meaning set forth in Article 2.1 of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: License shall have the meaning set forth in Article 2.2 of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text indicates that the definition of 'License' can be found in Article 2.1 of the Agreement. By changing it to 2.2 we are creating a In-Text contradiction because the reader would expect the definition of the License to be in Article 2.2. but in reality Article 2.2 talks about 'Restrictions', creating confusion about what the term actually represents, and creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee may extend the rights granted herein to register, import, export, store, handle, commercialize, promote, distribute and sell to its Affiliates and to its Distributors, provided that Licensee shall first provide to Licensor a written assurance from each of such Affiliate or Distributor to agree to be bound by, and to strictly comply with, all applicable terms, conditions, and obligations in this Agreement towards Licensor. Licensee shall not be entitled to assign the License or any of its rights under this Agreement or to grant any sub-licenses.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the location of the definition of 'License' from Article 2.1 to Article 2.2. Article 2.2 discusses 'Restrictions', so now the definition of the License is now associated with restrictions, when it originally wasn't. The Contradicted Text describes the usage and scope of the 'License', which is now undefined because the Changed Text has created confusion around where to find the meaning of that term. Therefore, this misalignment of terminology creates a strong in-text contradiction, as the reader cannot readily understand the scope of the license.

perturbation.original_text: The brand name for the Licensed Product in the Territory shall be Nolazol® (Brazilian trademark application No. 916475913 and in Mexico and elsewhere in the Territory to be provided by Licensor, collectively referred to as "Trademark") and may be modified by another name chosen and solely owned by the Licensor, subject to such modified name being communicated to Licensee no later than 6 months before the market launch of the License Product in Brazil.
perturbation.changed_text: The brand name for the Licensed Product in the Territory shall be Nolazol® (Brazilian trademark application No. 916475913 and in Mexico and elsewhere in the Territory to be provided by Licensor, collectively referred to as "Trademark") and may be modified by another name chosen and solely owned by the Licensee, subject to such modified name being communicated to Licensor no later than 6 months before the market launch of the License Product in Brazil.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Licensor owns the brand name and can modify it. The changed text says that the Licensee owns the brand name and can modify it. By doing this we are creating a In-Text Contradiction with Section 13 where says 'Both Parties acknowledge that all Intellectual Property existing at the Effective Date shall be and remain the sole property of the Licensor', the brand name is property of intellectual property so it can't be modified by the Licensee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both Parties acknowledge that all Intellectual Property existing at the Effective Date shall be and remain the sole property of the Licensor. All right, title and interest in and to any Developed Intellectual Property shall vest in and belong to the Licensor. The Licensee shall execute and deliver all reasonably necessary signatures and/or documents and take any further steps (or have his employees, agents and officers do the same) to the extent necessary to make any Developed Intellectual Property the sole property of the Licensor. The Licensee shall have right to use the Developed Intellectual Property for exploiting its rights granted in this Agreement until its expiration or termination as set forth in Article 19 hereunder. Licensee Know-how shall belong to the Licensee. The Licensee hereby grants to the Licensor an irrevocable, non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual, worldwide license to use of any Licensee Know-how.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text gives the Licensor the right to modify the brand name, consistent with the intellectual property ownership outlined in the contradicted text (Section 13). The changed text gives this right to the Licensee, which directly contradicts Section 13, which states that all Intellectual Property shall remain the sole property of the Licensor. Since a brand name is intellectual property, giving the Licensee the right to modify it clashes directly with the Licensor's sole ownership.
